audit_items.proof_trace: sass_make_error has exactly two allocations: `calloc` for the Sass_Value struct (`v`) and `sass_copy_c_string(msg)` for the message string. On the calloc-failure path, nothing is allocated. On the msg==NULL path, `v` is explicitly freed via `if (v->error.message == 0) { free(v); return 0; }`. On the success path, ownership is transferred to the caller via `return v`. Critically, `sass_copy_c_string` calls `sass_alloc_memory` (src/sass.cpp:37-45), which calls `exit(EXIT_FAILURE)` on OOM rather than returning NULL — so the string copy either succeeds or terminates the process, eliminating any intermediate leak scenario.
